summary: Large decompilation/matching PR for src/melee/gm/gmtou.c, the apparent Tournament game-mode file. It replaces multiple placeholder markers with C implementations, adds needed baselib/game-mode includes and externs, refines gmtou.static.h's TmAnimTimers layout, and receives a bot report showing four new matches plus several high-percentage improvements in main/melee/gm/gmtou.

key_files.role: Main Tournament game-mode decompilation target; adds includes, externs, typed access changes, and implementations for several previously placeholder functions.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:gmtou.c include additions and extern additions
diff_excerpt:placeholder replacements for fn_8019BA08, fn_8019C048, fn_8019C744, fn_8019D1BC, fn_8019DD60, gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame, gm_8019E634, gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter
text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report lists new matches and improvements for main/melee/gm/gmtou

key_files.role: Static header layout refinement for TmAnimTimers, including per-player arrays and per-player jobj/animation state struct.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:gmtou.static.h changes TmAnimTimers x0 from u16 pairs to u32, expands x6, adds x10/x18/x38 arrays, and renames x20[4] to x1D[4] with fields a/b/c/x0/x1/x2

what_changed: Converted lbl_804771B8 from an extern TmBoxArrays pointer to an extern TmBoxArrays object and updated archive loading from lbl_804771B8->boxN to &lbl_804771B8.boxN.
Added includes for fog, gobj gx link/object helpers, OS, dobj/mobj, and several gm headers so the new C bodies can use concrete APIs instead of relying on placeholders.
Added externs and a small local struct for data used by gmtou code, including lbl_804D663C, lbl_803DA2E0, gm_80477738, lbl_803B7D3C, lbl_804DA948, fn_80166CBC, and lbl_803B7D18.
Implemented fn_8019BA08 with child/next JObj traversal and translate-Z visibility selection based on tournament data and lbl_803DA2E0.x0.
Implemented fn_8019C048 for per-player display state, controller-error hiding, text visibility, animation-frame state updates, and transition from state 0 to 1 to 2.
Implemented fn_8019C744 for player object positioning, translate-Y animation lookup through lbl_803DA2E0.x20, and controller-error visibility handling.

Implemented a large fn_8019D1BC setup routine that creates multiple model GObjs per player, assigns callbacks, sets translations, initializes text objects, removes material animations from child JObjs, and precomputes Y translations into lbl_803DA2E0.x20.
Implemented fn_8019DD60 scene setup, including audio choice, camera/light/model setup, text setup, fog object setup, and TmAnimTimers initialization.

Implemented or partially matched larger frame/enter routines shown in the diff excerpt, including gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame and gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter with controller input, tournament data updates, audio preloading, and scene transition calls.
Refined TmAnimTimers layout in gmtou.static.h so code uses x1D[idx] per-player state fields rather than the previous x20[idx] layout.

smart_moves.text: Moved from placeholder bodies to structured C using project APIs such as HSD_JObjGetChild/GetNext, HSD_JObjSetTranslate*, HSD_SisLib, GObj_Create, fog setup, and audio helpers.
Refined a local struct layout in the static header and then updated call sites from lbl_80479A58.x20[idx].x0 to lbl_80479A58.x1D[idx].x0, reducing reliance on a mismatched guessed layout.
Used natural loops for repeated per-player and per-JObj traversal work, including for-loops over tournament players and child JObj siblings.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:fn_8019D1BC loops over tmd->x30 and tmd->x2E
diff_excerpt:gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame loops over tmd->x30 and tmd->x2E

smart_moves.text: Preserved address-style fn_/lbl_ names for uncertain symbols while giving local variables short role names such as tmd, gobj, jobj, text, state, start_frame, cur_frame, end_frame, and loop_flag.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a static state struct is clearly indexed per player, recovering the array layout can unlock cleaner call sites and improve matching; here the header changed from a vague x20 layout to x1D[4] per-player animation/state entries used throughout gmtou.c.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:lbl_80479A58.x1D[idx] uses in fn_8019C048, fn_8019C3EC, fn_8019C570, fn_8019C744, fn_8019CA38, fn_8019DD60
text_corpus:bot report shows .bss new match and several gmtou function improvements

decomp_lessons.lesson: For game-mode UI setup code, model-array indices and callback functions can be expressed with loops over player count and tournament entrants while still matching or nearly matching large setup routines.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:fn_8019D1BC creates per-player GObjs in a for-loop over tmd->x30 and names/text in a loop over tmd->x2E
text_corpus:bot report shows fn_8019D1BC improved from 0.00% to 98.71%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Controller-error paths in UI callbacks often pair JObj hidden flags with text scaling/visibility changes; keeping these paths explicit made fn_8019C048 and fn_8019C744 readable despite only one of them being fully matched in the bot report.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:fn_8019C048 HSD_PadMasterStatus err branch hides jobj and scales text to 0
diff_excerpt:fn_8019C744 controller-error branch clears hidden flags and repositions jobj
text_corpus:bot report shows fn_8019C744 new 100% match and fn_8019C048 84.12% improvement

naming_conventions.convention: Keep address-style global/function names when semantics are not proven, as seen with fn_8019BA08, fn_8019C048, lbl_80479A58, lbl_803DA2E0, and similar symbols.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:added functions and externs retain fn_/lbl_ address-style names

naming_conventions.convention: Use compact role names for local typed objects once the role is clear: tmd for TmData*, gobj for HSD_GObj*, jobj/c for HSD_JObj*, text for HSD_Text*, and state/frame names for animation state.
Retain x-offset field names in partially understood structs, but add scoped comments when a local role is supported; gmtou.static.h changes x1D[4] comment to 'per-player jobj/anim states'.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK in several functions to preserve stack shape while decompiling large routines.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: PAD_STACK is a narrow matching tactic; the bot report supports progress, but the PR evidence does not explain which stack pads are strictly required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Copied lbl_803B7D18 into a local table and indexed it as u16 triples to drive animation start/end/loop values.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:fn_8019C048 table[9] copy and ((u16*) table)[state * 3 + N] accesses
text_corpus:bot report shows fn_8019C048 improved to 84.12%, not fully matched
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Because fn_8019C048 is not reported as a full match, this table/cast shape should not be promoted as a general style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used gotos and labels inside gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame for found/not-found search flow in controller and tournament-entry logic.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:bot report shows gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame improved to 97.14%, not fully matched
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is likely a matching/control-flow tactic for a hard routine; no review evidence says it is desired general style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Mixed GET_JOBJ(gobj) with direct gobj->hsd_obj access depending on local codegen needs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The loaded standards warn GET_JOBJ should be evidence-led; this PR gives bot matching evidence but no author explanation for each access shape.

review_feedback.text: Improvements reported for fn_8019D1BC, gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame, gm_8019E634, fn_8019C048, gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter, .sdata2, gm_8019B8C4_OnEnter, .data, and .sdata.
No human review comments are present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: src/melee/gm/gmtou.static.h TmAnimTimers x1D per-player jobj anim states

evidence_refs: metadata_json complete for PR identity, changed file counts, and merge state
raw_diff/diff_excerpt provides substantial but truncated code changes
text_corpus includes PR body empty and one bot report
notes: PR body is empty, so author intent and validation beyond the bot report are not available.
The loaded raw diff is truncated in the prompt; targeted lookup recovered the gmtou.static.h hunk, but not every line of the large gmtou.c change was inspected.
Bot report provides strong evidence for four full matches and several improvements, but not for every matching tactic used in partially matched functions.
No human review feedback is available, so no reviewer intent or requested changes can be inferred.

accepted_candidate_records.title: gmtou TmAnimTimers layout recovery helped per-player animation state decompilation
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2423, gmtou.static.h changed TmAnimTimers from a rough x20 layout to x1D[4] per-player animation/state entries with a/b/c/x0/x1/x2 fields, and gmtou.c then consistently used lbl_80479A58.x1D[idx]. The bot report shows .bss newly matched and several gmtou functions improved or matched.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:bot report new .bss match and gmtou function improvements

accepted_candidate_records.title: gmtou matching used natural loops for repeated tournament player and JObj work
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2423 expresses repeated tournament UI setup with loops over player/entrant counts and JObj sibling traversal rather than leaving repeated generated blocks. This appears in fn_8019BA08 and fn_8019D1BC, with fn_8019BA08 reported as a new 100% match and fn_8019D1BC reported at 98.71%.

source_update_candidates.title: src/melee/gm/gmtou.c appears to be Tournament game-mode code
source_update_candidates.text: The file-level comment says the filename is a guess based on strings and 'Seems to be Tournament game code'; the diff uses assets such as GmTou1p, GmTou3p, GmTou4p, TmBox.dat, tournament_box2_array/3_array/4_array, and SIS_TournamentData.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:file comment near top of gmtou.c
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path classification for future searches, but the source itself labels the filename as a guess, so this should remain curator-reviewed rather than promoted directly.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not overgeneralize gmtou PAD_STACK/goto/table-copy tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2423 uses PAD_STACK, gotos, and a local copied table with a u16 cast in hard gmtou routines. The bot report supports progress, but only some affected functions are full matches; these tactics should remain evidence-led and narrow.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:PAD_STACK in fn_8019D1BC, fn_8019DD60, gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame, gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter
diff_excerpt:fn_8019C048 local table and ((u16*) table) accesses
text_corpus:bot report full matches for fn_8019BA08/fn_8019C744/fn_8019DD60 but partial improvements for fn_8019C048/gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame
source_update_candidates.reason: This aligns with the existing matching-tactics-need-evidence standard; curator may decide whether this PR is a useful example rather than a new standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all newly added functions fully matched; the bot report lists only fn_8019BA08, fn_8019C744, and fn_8019DD60 as new 100% function matches, while fn_8019D1BC, gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame, gm_8019E634, fn_8019C048, and gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter are improvements below 100%.
Do not infer human reviewer approval details or requested changes; no human reviews or review comments are loaded.
Do not promote the gmtou filename or exact high-level purpose as certain beyond the PR comment/assets; the source comment itself says the filename is a guess.
Do not promote PAD_STACK, goto labels, GET_JOBJ choice, or local table casting as general accepted style from this PR alone.
